text:
The first space feels empty and almost sterile, its wide openness filled with anticipation, but also a hint of unease—like waiting for something to begin in a place that is both inviting and distant. The harsh lines and bright lights emphasize my solitude.

The second space, in contrast, immerses me in color and information. Surrounded by vibrant shapes and thought-provoking text, I feel curiosity blossoming alongside a sense of disorientation. Here, I am drawn into inquiry, the boundaries between human and machine, art and knowledge, blurring and exciting me. I feel more engaged, though also humbled by the complexity swirling around me.
